Source_Species.reason: CfPOR was identified from the C. forskohlii root cork transcriptome.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper consistently refers to Coleus forskohlii as the source species.

Applied_Species.reason: CfPOR was cloned for co-integration in yeast; no other applied species is described for CfPOR.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied host for CfPOR is stated as S. cerevisiae.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly calls CfPOR a 'NADPH-dependent cytochrome P450 oxidoreductase'.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states a sequence encoding CfPOR was identified from the root cork transcriptome and that it was the only one present there.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: CfPOR was cloned for co-integration in yeast to support P450 activity; this is explicitly stated.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: CfPOR is included in the engineered yeast pathway producing forskolin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Forskolin is explicitly described as a labdane-type diterpenoid, i.e. a terpenoid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The abstract states forskolin is a cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Forskolin is said to accumulate in the root cork/root cork cells of C. forskohlii.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: CfPOR is described as required to support P450 activity in the yeast reconstruction of forskolin biosynthesis, but direct gene-by-gene necessity was not separately tested.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point matches Results: CfPOR was the only POR in root cork transcriptome, was cloned for yeast co-integration to support P450s, and was part of the heterologous pathway yielding forskolin.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transcriptome identification, cloning, co-integration in yeast, and metabolite monitoring of transformed strains are all described.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: CfPOR was introduced specifically to support the activity of the co-expressed forskolin-pathway P450s CfCYP76AH15, CfCYP76AH11 and CfCYP76AH16 in yeast, but direct physical interaction was not tested.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Forskolin production in yeast was monitored during glucose-limited fermentation.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper discusses optimizing CYP expression/interactions in yeast for improved yields, but does not specifically frame CfPOR alone as an engineering target/value statement in these exact terms.

Omics_Data.reason: The root cork RNA-seq and SRA accession SAMN06013363 are explicitly given.

Other_Important_Info.reason: CfPOR is presented as the reductase partner needed to support P450 activity, not as an enzyme directly introducing a metabolite structural change.

Source_Species.reason: CfACT1-6 was identified from the C. forskohlii root cork transcriptome.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is Coleus forskohlii throughout.

Applied_Species.reason: CfACT1-6 was functionally tested by transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The transient host is explicitly N. benthamiana.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: ACT candidates were mined from clade III of the BAHD family, and the discussion says ACT1-6 and ACT1-8 belong to clade III.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: It was identified from the root cork transcriptome, but no gene-specific expression pattern beyond that is shown in Results.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: CfACT1-6 was functional upon transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves producing deacetylforskolin.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: CfACT1-6 yields some forskolin among acetylated products.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Forskolin is described as a cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Forskolin accumulation in root cork is stated.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper explicitly states CfACT1-6 acetylated 13b and produced a broad range of acetylated products of which forskolin was a minor fraction.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly described in Results and Figure 8.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Candidate mining from root cork transcriptome, transient co-expression in N. benthamiana producing deacetylforskolin, and LC-MS analysis are described.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves was the testing system.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Non-specific acetylated side products were explicitly observed.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper shows it is inferior for specific forskolin production and could contribute to product diversity, but this engineering-value phrasing is not explicit.

Omics_Data.reason: Root cork RNA-seq and accession SAMN06013363 are provided for the transcriptome from which ACT candidates were mined.

Other_Important_Info.reason: CfACT1-6 was functionally validated in Results as an alternative acetyltransferase affecting forskolin-related product profile.

Source_Species.reason: CfCYP76AH8 is a C. forskohlii gene.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is Coleus forskohlii.

Applied_Species.reason: CfCYP76AH8 was tested by transient expression in N. benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Nicotiana benthamiana is the applied species used for functional assays.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CfCYP76AH8 is explicitly assigned to the CYP76AH subfamily.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Members of the CYP76AH family, including identified members such as CfCYP76AH8, were said to be highly or exclusively expressed in root cork cells.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It showed catalytic activity after transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: CfCYP76AH8 contributes to formation of forskolin-pathway intermediates and can participate in deacetylforskolin formation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Forskolin's therapeutic cyclic-AMP-booster role is stated in the paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state CfCYP76AH8 formed mainly 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide and also 1,11-dihydroxy-13R-manoyl oxide; triplet assays including AH8 produced deacetylforskolin, though highest amounts were with AH15/AH11/AH16.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These points match the reported product profile and triplet-combination experiments.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transcriptome candidate selection, expression studies, transient expression, GC-MS/LC-qTOF-MS, and NMR identification are all described.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: CfCYP76AH8 was co-expressed with CfCYP76AH11 and CfCYP76AH16 in triplet assays, but direct protein interaction was not demonstrated.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana leaves is the experimental condition.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper reports multiple di- and trihydroxylated products and lower specificity than CfCYP76AH15.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Its promiscuity suggests utility in combinatorial diterpenoid engineering, but this is an interpretive application rather than a direct tested result for AH8 specifically.

Omics_Data.reason: Root cork RNA-seq and accession SAMN06013363 are explicitly reported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: It is not part of the most specific minimal pathway; the optimal triplet used AH15/AH11/AH16.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Coleus forskohlii is the stated source species.

Applied_Species.reason: CfCYP76AH17 was assayed in transiently transformed N. benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: N. benthamiana is the assay host described.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CfCYP76AH17 is discussed with the CYP76AH enzymes, but it is not among the seven transcripts initially assigned to CYP76AH from the root cork transcriptome description. Still, later Results treat it as a CYP76AH enzyme; subfamily assignment is supported.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The statement 'identified members of the CYP76AH family were highly or exclusively expressed in the root cork cells' supports this only if CfCYP76AH17 is included, but AH17 is not named in the earlier list of seven transcriptome-selected CYP76AH members. Support is therefore incomplete.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It was active upon transient expression in N. benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: CfCYP76AH17 participates in production of deacetylforskolin with other CYPs, connecting it to forskolin biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Forskolin is described as a cyclic AMP booster used in glaucoma and heart failure treatment.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Forskolin accumulates in root cork/root cork cells.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state CfCYP76AH17 formed 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide as main product and also 1,11-dihydroxy-13R-manoyl oxide; triplet combinations including AH17 with AH11 and AH16 produced deacetylforskolin.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These three summary claims follow directly from Figures 2 and 5 and the accompanying text.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transient expression, GC-MS/LC-qTOF-MS, and NMR are documented; however qRT-PCR expression analysis for AH17 specifically is not clearly shown from the text provided.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: AH17 was co-expressed with AH11 and AH16, but direct molecular interaction was not demonstrated.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves is supported.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper documents promiscuous formation of multiple higher oxygenated products, reducing pathway specificity.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Possible as a combinatorial engineering enzyme by inference, but not explicitly evaluated for application value.

Omics_Data.reason: The study includes root cork RNA-seq with SRA accession SAMN06013363, but whether AH17 itself comes from that exact dataset is not fully clear from the provided text.

Other_Important_Info.reason: It was not chosen as the optimal C-11 oxidase in the minimal pathway; that role was assigned to AH15.

Source_Species.reason: CfCYP76AH15 is a Coleus forskohlii gene.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Coleus forskohlii is the explicit source species.

Applied_Species.reason: CfCYP76AH15 was tested in N. benthamiana and later stably expressed in S. cerevisiae.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae are explicitly used.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CfCYP76AH15 is assigned to the CYP76AH subfamily.

Enzyme_Name.reason: As a CYP, it is a cytochrome P450 monooxygenase; this is directly implied by the paper's terminology.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given in the paper for CfCYP76AH15.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: CfCYP76AH15 is part of forskolin biosynthesis and more broadly labdane diterpenoid biosynthesis from 13R-manoyl oxide.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Its characterized activity is on the 13R-manoyl oxide-derived branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: It catalyzes an early oxygenation step from 13R-manoyl oxide to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide; 'upstream to midstream' is a fair inference.

Primary_Substrate.reason: 13R-manoyl oxide is explicit; miltiradiene is also explicitly tested and converted. The phrasing 'miltiradiene' is supported better than 'dehydroabietadiene route'.

Primary_Product.reason: 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide and ferruginol are explicitly reported products.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper explicitly shows efficient specific conversion of 13R-manoyl oxide to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide and conversion of miltiradiene to ferruginol. Mention of 'dehydroabietadiene route' is broader than directly shown, but overall description is supported.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: It forms a pathway intermediate required for target product formation.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: CfCYP76AH15 is part of the minimal pathway to forskolin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states forskolin is a cyclic AMP booster used in glaucoma and heart failure treatment.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Forskolin accumulates in root cork/root cork cells.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Members of the CYP76AH family were highly or exclusively expressed in root cork cells, including AH15 from the root cork transcriptome.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: AH15 was active in transient N. benthamiana assays and co-expressed in engineered yeast.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: This matches the core Results: efficient C-11 keto formation and inclusion in the minimal three-P450 pathway to deacetylforskolin/forskolin.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary statements are explicitly supported by the Results and figures.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in the paper for AH15 or the pathway containing AH15.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: CfPOR, CfCYP76AH11, and CfCYP76AH16 were co-expressed with AH15 in pathway reconstruction, but direct physical interaction was not tested.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Both transient expression in N. benthamiana and glucose-limited yeast fermentation are explicitly described.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly says one or several P450s limit flux in yeast, but does not assign this specifically to AH15.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Intermediate accumulation in yeast is documented, but attributing it specifically to AH15 is broader than shown; the paper frames it as one or several P450s/overall imbalance.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports AH15 as a key enzyme for pathway reconstruction and discusses optimizing CYP panels/expression for diterpenoid engineering.

Omics_Data.reason: Root cork RNA-seq and accession SAMN06013363 are provided.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states ferruginol was detected in root cork and that CfCYP76AH15 may also be involved in ferruginol biosynthesis in planta; similarity to rosemary CYP76AH4 is also discussed.

corrections.reason: No EC number is given in the paper for CfCYP76AH15.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: AH11 acts after initial scaffold formation to add oxygenation pattern required mid-pathway.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: AH11 contributes the oxygenation pattern toward the final target product.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: AH11 is part of the minimal pathway to forskolin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Forskolin's cyclic AMP booster medicinal role is explicit.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: CYP76AH members including AH11 were highly or exclusively expressed in root cork cells.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: AH11 was functionally active in transient N. benthamiana and used successfully in yeast.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper supports AH11 as providing complementary oxygenations required for deacetylforskolin/forskolin production.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are supported, though the precise phrase 'mainly accounts' comes from the Discussion rather than a single direct assay.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transcriptome mining, expression studies, transient expression, GC-MS/LC-qTOF-MS, NMR of intermediates, and stable yeast integration are all described.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: AH11 was co-expressed with CfPOR, AH15 and AH16, but direct protein-protein interaction was not shown.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Both transient plant expression and glucose-limited yeast fermentation are documented.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish AH11 itself as rate-limiting.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The general statement about P450 imbalance in yeast is supported, but assigning it specifically to AH11 is not directly shown.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: As an essential member of the minimal pathway, AH11 is relevant for engineering; optimization of CYP expression/kinetics is discussed generally.

Omics_Data.reason: Root cork RNA-seq and accession SAMN06013363 are reported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Multifunctionality and partial redundancy among CYP76AH enzymes, including AH11, are explicitly discussed.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: C-9 hydroxylation is a midstream oxygenation step in the pathway.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This C-9 hydroxylation to 3a/coleorol is explicitly described and linked to the C-9 oxygenation in forskolin.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: AH16 is part of the minimal pathway to forskolin.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported by the single-enzyme and triplet-combination data.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: High 3a accumulation in yeast is explicitly shown, but whether this is a 'tradeoff' of AH16 itself versus downstream limitations is interpretive.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: As an essential enzyme for complete pathway reconstruction and a locus of flux imbalance, this application value is supported by the pathway-engineering context.

Source_Species.reason: CfACT1-8 was identified from the C. forskohlii root cork transcriptome.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is Coleus forskohlii.

Applied_Species.reason: CfACT1-8 was functionally tested in N. benthamiana and then co-expressed in S. cerevisiae.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae are explicit applied species.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: ACT candidates were mined from clade III BAHD family, and the discussion explicitly says ACT1-8 belongs to clade III.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper identifies it as an acetyltransferase/ACT catalyzing acetylation.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given in the paper for CfACT1-8.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It catalyzes the final acetylation step to forskolin.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The paper explicitly describes acetylation of deacetylforskolin as the final step of forskolin biosynthesis.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Deacetylforskolin is explicit, but acetyl-CoA is inferred from BAHD family behavior and not directly shown as a tested substrate in the Results for CfACT1-8.

Primary_Product.reason: CfACT1-8 efficiently converts deacetylforskolin to forskolin.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper explicitly states regiospecific acetylation of the C-7 hydroxyl group of deacetylforskolin to form forskolin; acetyl-CoA use is inferred from BAHD family context rather than directly assayed.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: CfACT1-8 directly forms the target product forskolin.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Forskolin is the direct product of CfACT1-8 activity.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper describes forskolin as a cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Forskolin accumulates in root cork/root cork cells.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: CfACT1-8 was identified from the root cork transcriptome; no stronger gene-specific expression pattern is given in Results.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It was functional in transient N. benthamiana assays and used in engineered yeast.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Results explicitly state high activity and specificity with efficient conversion of 13b to forskolin and absence of detectable acetylated side products.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly stated in Results and Figures 8–10.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transcriptome mining, 5'RACE, transient co-expression in N. benthamiana, LC-MS/authentic standards, and stable yeast integration are all described.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Both transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana and glucose-limited fermentation in yeast are explicitly described.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The discussion states low deacetylforskolin accumulation shows CfACT1-8 is not a limiting step, so this field is supported.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Compared with CfACT1-6, few side products were detected and no major penalty was reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper strongly supports ACT1-8 as the preferred terminal enzyme for precise engineering of forskolin production.

Omics_Data.reason: Root cork RNA-seq and accession SAMN06013363 are reported for the transcriptome used to identify ACT candidates.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The discussion explicitly notes ACT1-8 belongs to clade III BAHDs, distinct from taxol-related clade V diterpenoid acyltransferases.

corrections.reason: No EC number is given in the paper for CfACT1-8.
